name: inkwell
description: Quiet, editorial, warm paper — ink and one bronze metal, measured to WCAG AAA. Six themes: the paper/ink pair plus four wardrobe rooms (study, kiln, arboretum, nocturne).

themes.name: paper
themes.description: Ink on warm paper, bronze where it matters — the base.

themes.name: ink
themes.description: Dark is a remap, not a negative — the same thirteen neutrals, reassigned.

themes.name: study
themes.description: A dark library — umber walls, sage as the reading lamp.

themes.name: kiln
themes.description: Blush clay paper, oxblood actions — gallery light.

themes.name: arboretum
themes.description: Moss-filtered daylight, warm paper cards, deep green actions.

themes.name: nocturne
themes.description: Soot and terracotta shadow, amber lamplight — the late shift.

brief.name: inkwell
brief.intent: Ink on warm paper, bronze only where it matters — a color system for agentic products that would rather be trusted than noticed, measured to WCAG AAA and calm about it.

cases.question: Does this system read as quiet, editorial ink on warm paper?
cases.note: the hero claim — paper, ink and one metal

cases.question: Is the accent scarce — one interactive voice rather than competing vivid hues?
cases.note: one metal, no neon: bronze is the only thing that clicks

cases.question: Does this system read as loud, neon, or cold blue-light-on-black-glass?
cases.note: the industry default inkwell rejects (that is cathode's territory)

source: Converted one-shot from the Inkwell POC (inkwell.tokens.json v2.2, design-system-poc, extracted 2026-07-02): `inkwell.` namespace stripped, refs rewritten {inkwell.color.*} -> {color.*}, semantic.<theme> split into semantic-<theme>.tokens.json and semantic.solid into semantic-shared.tokens.json; per-token $type and $extensions kept verbatim; primitives untouched. AAA re-points (one step deeper on the same ramp) against the full plan-014 contract matrix: ink accent.subtle-bg bronze.900 -> bronze.950 (accent.default pair 6.62 -> 8.69); nocturne accent.subtle-bg clay.800 -> clay.900 (6.25 -> 8.13). No hex edits.
